title: Access to clean fuels and technologies for cooking (% of population)
definition: Access to clean fuels and technologies for cooking is the proportion of total population primarily using clean cooking fuels and technologies for cooking. Under WHO guidelines, kerosene is excluded from clean cooking fuels.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include United States, Uruguay, Trinidad and Tobago, while the lightest include South Sudan, Burundi, Guinea-Bissau; that contrast is the fastest way to read the clean cooking fuels access pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Clean cooking access captures a daily energy and health burden that electricity maps can miss.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: GDP per capita, PPP can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
GDP per capita can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Energy maps often separate access, consumption, and production mix.
Look for hydro, fossil-fuel, and low-access signals.

reviewNotes: A household-energy map with a real public-health story.
High income correlation requires careful distractors.
